Function: conj_grad(int*, int*, double*, double*, double*, double*, double*, double*, double*) Module: cg.C Source: cg.cpp:519-700 [...] Coverage (incl. loops): 97.50% | (excl. loops): 0.01% 
/home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp: 519 - 700
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


519: 		double* rnorm){


520: 	int j, k;


521: 	int cgit, cgitmax;


522: 	double alpha, beta, suml;


523: 	static double d, sum, rho, rho0;


524: 	const int nrow = lastrow - firstrow + 1;


525: 	const int ncol = lastcol - firstcol + 1;


[...]


538: 	#pragma omp single nowait


539: 	{


540: 


541: 		rho = 0.0;


542: 		sum = 0.0;


543: 	}


544: 	/* initialize the CG algorithm */


545: 	#pragma omp for


546: 	for(j = 0; j < naa+1; j++){


547: 		q_l[j] = 0.0;


548: 		z_l[j] = 0.0;


549: 		r_l[j] = x_l[j];


550: 		p_l[j] = r_l[j];


[...]


559: 	#pragma omp for reduction(+:rho)


560: 	for(j = 0; j < ncol; j++){


561: 		rho += r_l[j]*r_l[j];


562: 	}


563: 


564: 	/* the conj grad iteration loop */


565: 	for(cgit = 1; cgit <= cgitmax; cgit++){


[...]


580: 		#pragma omp single nowait


581: 		{


582: 			d = 0.0;


[...]


588: 			rho0 = rho;


589: 			rho = 0.0;


590: 		}


591: 


592: 		#pragma omp for nowait schedule(static)


593: 		for(j = 0; j < nrow; j++){


594: 			suml = 0.0;


595: 			const int row_start = rowstr_l[j];


596: 			const int row_end = rowstr_l[j+1];


597: 			const int len = row_end - row_start;


598: 			const int* __restrict ci = colidx_l + row_start;


599: 			const double* __restrict av = a_l + row_start;


600: 			#pragma omp simd reduction(+:suml)


601: 			for(k = 0; k < len; k++){


602: 				const int idx = ci[k];


603: 				suml += av[k] * p_l[idx];


604: 			}


605: 			q_l[j] = suml;


[...]


614: 		#pragma omp for reduction(+:d)


615: 		for (j = 0; j < ncol; j++) {


616: 			d += p_l[j]*q_l[j];


[...]


624: 		alpha = rho0 / d;


[...]


633: 		#pragma omp for reduction(+:rho)


634: 		for(j = 0; j < ncol; j++){


635: 			z_l[j] += alpha*p_l[j];


636: 			r_l[j] -= alpha*q_l[j];


[...]


644: 			rho += r_l[j]*r_l[j];


[...]


652: 		beta = rho / rho0;


[...]


659: 		#pragma omp for


660: 		for(j = 0; j < ncol; j++){


661: 			p_l[j] = r_l[j] + beta*p_l[j];


[...]


672: 	#pragma omp for nowait schedule(static)


673: 	for(j = 0; j < nrow; j++){


674: 		suml = 0.0;


675: 		const int row_start = rowstr_l[j];


676: 		const int row_end = rowstr_l[j+1];


677: 		const int len = row_end - row_start;


678: 		const int* __restrict ci = colidx_l + row_start;


679: 		const double* __restrict av = a_l + row_start;


680: 		#pragma omp simd reduction(+:suml)


681: 		for(k = 0; k < len; k++){


682: 			const int idx = ci[k];


683: 			suml += av[k] * z_l[idx];


684: 		}


685: 		r_l[j] = suml;


[...]


693: 	#pragma omp for reduction(+:sum)


694: 	for(j = 0; j < ncol; j++){


695: 		suml   = x_l[j] - r_l[j];


696: 		sum += suml*suml;


697: 	}


698: 	#pragma omp single


699: 		*rnorm = sqrt(sum);


700: }

Source file and linescg.cpp:519-700

          
Modulecg.C

        

        
The function is defined in /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:519-525,538-550,559-565,580-582,588-605,614-616,624,633-636,644,652,659-661,672-685,693-700.

Code clean check


Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation).
By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 84.00 to 44.00 cycles (1.91x speedup).

Workaround


  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)


Vectorization


Your function is poorly vectorized.
Only 40% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).
By fully vectorizing your function, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 84.00 to 48.19 cycles (1.74x speedup).

Details


27% of SSE/AVX instructions are used in vector version (process two or more data elements in vector registers):

  • 41% of SSE/AVX loads are used in vector version.
  • 33% of SSE/AVX stores are used in vector version.
  • 0% of SSE/AVX divide and square root instructions are used in vector version.
  • 10% of SSE/AVX instructions that are not load, store, addition, subtraction nor multiply instructions are used in vector version.
Since your execution units are vector units, only a fully vectorized function can use their full power.


Workaround


  • Try another compiler or update/tune your current one
  • Make array accesses unit-stride:

    • If your function streams arrays of structures (AoS), try to use structures of arrays instead (SoA):
for(i) a[i].x = b[i].x; (slow, non stride 1) => for(i) a.x[i] = b.x[i]; (fast, stride 1)


Execution units bottlenecks


Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.


  

No data for this section

  


CALL instructions


Detected function call instructions.


Details


Calling (and then returning from) a function prevents many compiler optimizations (like vectorization), breaks control flow (which reduces pipeline performance) and executes extra instructions to save/restore the registers used inside it, which is very expensive (dozens of cycles). Consider to inline small functions.

  • unknown: 3 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:538
    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:545
    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:545


Complex instructions


Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.


Details


These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.

  • IDIV: 1 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:546
  • JMP: 7 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:550

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:550


Workaround


Use vector aligned instructions:

  1. align your arrays on 32 bytes boundaries: replace { void *p = malloc (size); } with { void *p; posix_memalign (&p, 32, size); }.
  2. inform your compiler that your arrays are vector aligned: if array 'foo' is 32 bytes-aligned, define a pointer 'p_foo' as __builtin_assume_aligned (foo, 32) and use it instead of 'foo' in the loop.




Conversion instructions


Detected expensive conversion instructions.

Details


  • CLTD: 1 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:546
  • CLTQ: 1 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:546


Workaround


Avoid mixing data with different types. In particular, check if the type of constants is the same as array elements.

Type of elements and instruction set


No instructions are processing arithmetic or math operations on FP elements. This function is probably writing/copying data or processing integer elements.

Matching between your function (in the source code) and the binary function


The binary function does not contain any FP arithmetical operations.
The binary function is loading 520 bytes.
The binary function is storing 1308 bytes.

Code clean check


Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation).
By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 84.00 to 44.00 cycles (1.91x speedup).

Workaround


  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)


Vectorization


Your function is poorly vectorized.
Only 40% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).
By fully vectorizing your function, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 84.00 to 48.19 cycles (1.74x speedup).

Details


27% of SSE/AVX instructions are used in vector version (process two or more data elements in vector registers):

  • 41% of SSE/AVX loads are used in vector version.
  • 33% of SSE/AVX stores are used in vector version.
  • 0% of SSE/AVX divide and square root instructions are used in vector version.
  • 10% of SSE/AVX instructions that are not load, store, addition, subtraction nor multiply instructions are used in vector version.
Since your execution units are vector units, only a fully vectorized function can use their full power.


Workaround


  • Try another compiler or update/tune your current one
  • Make array accesses unit-stride:

    • If your function streams arrays of structures (AoS), try to use structures of arrays instead (SoA):
for(i) a[i].x = b[i].x; (slow, non stride 1) => for(i) a.x[i] = b.x[i]; (fast, stride 1)


Execution units bottlenecks


Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.


  

No data for this section

  


CALL instructions


Detected function call instructions.


Details


Calling (and then returning from) a function prevents many compiler optimizations (like vectorization), breaks control flow (which reduces pipeline performance) and executes extra instructions to save/restore the registers used inside it, which is very expensive (dozens of cycles). Consider to inline small functions.

  • unknown: 3 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:538
    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:545
    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:545


Complex instructions


Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.


Details


These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.

  • IDIV: 1 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:546

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:550


Workaround


Use vector aligned instructions:

  1. align your arrays on 32 bytes boundaries: replace { void *p = malloc (size); } with { void *p; posix_memalign (&p, 32, size); }.
  2. inform your compiler that your arrays are vector aligned: if array 'foo' is 32 bytes-aligned, define a pointer 'p_foo' as __builtin_assume_aligned (foo, 32) and use it instead of 'foo' in the loop.




Conversion instructions


Detected expensive conversion instructions.

Details


  • CLTD: 1 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:546
  • CLTQ: 1 occurrences►

    • /home/platy/Desktop/NPB-CG-CPP/NPB-OMP/CG/cg.cpp:546


Workaround


Avoid mixing data with different types. In particular, check if the type of constants is the same as array elements.

Type of elements and instruction set


No instructions are processing arithmetic or math operations on FP elements. This function is probably writing/copying data or processing integer elements.

Matching between your function (in the source code) and the binary function


The binary function does not contain any FP arithmetical operations.
The binary function is loading 520 bytes.
The binary function is storing 1308 bytes.
